methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Terraform Industries, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Casey Handmer, is valued in this analysis against a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA). The company has raised $26M to date.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $26M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yields $52M. Peer-median analysis draws on three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—which yield a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. The disparity between these methods reflects Terraform's earlier funding position relative to slightly more mature peers in the climate tech space.

Triangulating across both approaches produces a valuation range of $44M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $101M. The stage-progression method suggests the lower bound, while peer multiples anchor the upper range. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation within the crowded frontier-energy sector, path to revenue, and whether peer multiples will sustain in a tightening venture environment. Investors should also assess Terraform's capital intensity relative to its Series A raise and peer burn profiles.
